patch.value: Stale-lock takeover race in acquireLock: two waiters can both remove the stale lock and both believe they own the item

handleExistingLock (src/core/lock/lock.ts) is check-then-act: waiter A and waiter B both read the lock file, both classify it stale (TTL expired), then both unlink+create. Interleaving: A unlinks stale → A creates fresh lock (wx succeeds) → B (which read the OLD stale payload before A's create) unlinks A's FRESH lock → B creates its own → A and B hold the "lock" simultaneously and both mutate the item; the loser's writeFileAtomic is silently overwritten. Window is small (only opens when a stale lock is contended, e.g. after a crashed process, right when parallel agents pile on) but it is exactly the extreme case the lock exists for. Fix options: (a) after createLockFile succeeds following a stale takeover, read the lock back and verify pid+owner+created_at match our payload, else treat as lost and re-loop; (b) takeover-by-rename: rename the stale lock to a unique tombstone (rename fails for the second waiter → ENOENT → re-loop) before creating the fresh lock; (c) compare-and-unlink guard via fs.stat inode/mtime captured at read time. Same pattern exists in the retry loop's 3-attempt cap: with N>3 waiters on a stale lock, some callers exhaust attempts and get a generic 'Failed to acquire lock' — fold into the pm-2muu wait-budget loop when both land.
Stale-lock takeover is single-winner (atomic rename or compare-and-delete); a race test with two concurrent waiters yields exactly one lock owner.

## Context
Filed by the 2026-07-03 concurrency audit. Stale-lock takeover is check-then-act: two waiters can both classify a lock stale, both unlink, both create — and both believe they own the item; the loser's write is silently overwritten.

## Interleaving
A reads stale → B reads stale → A unlinks + creates fresh lock → B (holding the OLD stale read) unlinks A's FRESH lock → B creates its own → A and B mutate concurrently. Window opens only when a stale lock (crashed process) is contended by parallel agents — exactly the recovery case the lock exists for.

## Relation to pm-2muu (dep: related)
The uncommitted pm-2muu rework folds stale handling into the wait loop and caps removals (MAX_STALE_LOCK_REMOVALS=3), which bounds livelock but does NOT close this race: the unlink target is still not identity-checked.

## Approach
Make removal identity-safe: only remove the exact lock observed (compare payload/inode — e.g. rename the stale lock to a unique tombstone name and verify, or re-read after create and back off if the payload isn't ours). After createLockFile succeeds following a stale removal, re-read the lock file and verify owner == self; if not, treat as lost and re-enter the wait loop.

## Definition of done
A deterministic two-waiter stale-lock test (fake clock / injected reads) shows exactly one winner; stress run over crashed-lock scenarios shows no double-grant; evidence recorded here.
